commit.message:
fix(cli): sync --min-history default/fallback with MIN_EVENTS=20

--min-history argparser default was hardcoded 10; fallback in
_run_self_calibration also used 10. Both now match MIN_EVENTS=20
from self_calibrator.py.

Without this fix, slop-detector --self-calibrate (no flag) triggers
calibration at 10 events, contradicting the documented 20-event threshold
and reducing statistical reliability.

commit.message:
Fix deterministic MountVolume test failures on ARM64 Helix machines (#126660)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was created with Copilot assistance.

## Fix deterministic MountVolume test failures on ARM64 Helix machines

Fixes #125295, fixes #125624, fixes #126627

### Problem

`Directory_Delete_MountVolume.RunTest` and
`Directory_ReparsePoints_MountVolume.runTest` fail deterministically
(~100% of the time, ~750ms duration) on the `Windows.11.Arm64.Open`
Helix machine pool. This is **not timing-related** and was not addressed
by the delay/polling fixes in #125914 or the Unmount resilience fix in
#125625 (those PRs fixed real timing issues -- pre-fix failures on other
configurations have since expired from AzDO retention, so we can't
verify directly, but there is no evidence they were ineffective for
their intended purpose).

**Root cause**: The ARM64 Helix machines have an E:\ drive (likely an
Azure resource/temp disk) that passes all `DriveInfo` checks --
`DriveType=Fixed`, `DriveFormat=NTFS`, `IsReady=True` -- but
`GetVolumeNameForVolumeMountPoint` fails with `ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER`
(87). The drive has no volume GUID and doesn't support volume mount
point operations. `IOServices.GetNtfsDriveOtherThanCurrent()` returns
this drive, and the test crashes trying to use it.

Some ARM64 Helix machines have only C:\ and a CD-ROM (no second drive at
all). On those machines, the cross-drive scenarios already skip
gracefully and only same-drive scenarios 3.x run.

### Evidence

Analyzed Helix console logs from 5 post-fix builds (all
`arm64-NativeAOT-Win11`, same C:\ volume GUID). Every failure shows the
identical pattern:
- Scenario 1: `GetVolumeNameForVolumeMountPoint("E:\")` -> error 87
- Scenario 2: `SetVolumeMountPoint` onto E:\ succeeds but path traversal
through the mount point fails with `DirectoryNotFoundException`
- Scenarios 3.x (same-drive): Always pass

Reproduced locally by removing the real E: drive letter and creating
`SUBST E:` which exhibits identical error 87 behavior.

### Changes

1. **`IOServices.GetNtfsDriveOtherThan()`**: After the existing
Fixed/Ready/NTFS checks, also verify the drive has a volume GUID via
`GetVolumeNameForVolumeMountPoint`. Drives without one (SUBST drives,
Azure resource disks) are skipped.

2. **`DumpDriveInformation` diagnostic test**: New Helix-only test
(following the `DescriptionNameTests.DumpRuntimeInformationToConsole`
pattern) that dumps all drives with their volume GUIDs to the console
log. Makes future drive-related CI issues immediately diagnosable from
the same Helix work item log.

3. **`GetVolumeNameForVolumeMountPoint` P/Invoke in DllImports.cs**:
Uses `char[]` (not `StringBuilder`) because this file uses
`LibraryImport` which does not support `StringBuilder`.

### Local validation

| Scenario | Before fix | After fix |
|---|---|---|
| SUBST E: (no volume GUID) | Error 87 / DirectoryNotFoundException |
Pass (SUBST filtered, scenarios 3.x run) |
| Real NTFS E: | Pass (all scenarios) | Pass (all scenarios) |
| Single-drive machine | Scenarios 1/2 skip, 3.x pass | Same -- no
change |

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values
- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit
- Settings reset audit
- Enable/disable audit
- No audit on validation failure
- Audit failure resilience (request still succeeds)

fix: bypass manual-open check when sun clears obstacle threshold (#49)

* fix: bypass manual-open check when sun clears obstacle threshold

When a cover is in obstacle-blocked (no-sun) mode the cover is raised
to 100%. When the sun then rises above the obstacle threshold, the
manual-open check would see position=100% and refuse to drive the
cover back down, leaving it raised indefinitely.

Fix: track per-cover which entities were obstacle-blocked last cycle
(_obstacle_was_blocking set). On the first cycle after sun clears the
threshold, bypass the manual-open check — same pattern as
_first_facade_hit_this_cycle used for the sunrise case.

commit.message:
fix: add /load-more HTTP endpoint to ChatWebServer for PWA history pagination

The web PWA calls `POST /load-more` via `_bridge.loadMore()` in web-app.ts,
but ChatWebServer.registerContexts() never registered this route, causing a 404
on every load-more click in the web UI.

Added:
- `onLoadMore: Runnable` volatile field to ChatWebServer alongside the other
  action callbacks
- `/load-more` context registration that calls `onLoadMore` when the route is hit
- Wire-up in ChatToolWindowContent.createResponsePanel() so load-more triggers
  `onLoadMoreHistory()` on the EDT (same pattern as onStop, onDisconnect, etc.)

The JCEF panel uses a native JBCefJSQuery bridge for load-more, so it is
unaffected. The web path now mirrors the JCEF path through the same
`onLoadMoreHistory()` handler and relies on `executeJs` → `pushJsEvent` to
deliver the `prependBatch` / `showLoadMore` JS events to all SSE clients.

   - session_start.ts: requestApproval rejected hints marked 'in use'
   - session-manager.ts: getAvailableColors didn't prioritize hints
   - approve_agent.ts: didn't access pending agent's colorHint
   - agent-approval.ts: PendingApproval had no colorHint field

   - Added sessionApproval config field (governor|manual|auto)
   - Auto-enable delegation on startup when governor mode configured

4. approve_agent always visible (agent-approval.ts)
   - Removed dynamic enable/disable — tool always available

All fixes live-tested in production MCP (PID 19564, port 3099).
